commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

commit.message:
feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.

commit.message:
Implement pronominal prefix AGR/POSS distinction

Based on Henderson (1965):
- ka-/na-/a- on VERBS = agreement markers (1SG, 2SG, 3SG)
- ka-/na-/a- on NOUNS = possessive markers (1SG.POSS, 2SG.POSS, 3SG.POSS)

Implementation:
- Added post-processing step in analyze_word() to detect noun following prefix
- Uses heuristics to identify nouns: NOUN_STEMS membership, semantic content
- Uses heuristics to identify verbs: VERB_STEMS, VERB_STEM_PAIRS, verbal glosses
- Only changes gloss when noun follows and verb does NOT follow

Examples:
- kainn: '1SG-house' → '1SG.POSS-house' (ka- + noun)
- kanei: '1SG-have' (unchanged, ka- + verb)
- kaneih: '1SG-have.II' (unchanged, ka- + Form II verb)

Added tests/test_prefix_agr_poss.py:
- Tests prefix + noun → POSS (3 cases)
- Tests prefix + verb → AGR (2 cases)
- Tests prefix + Form II verb → AGR (2 cases)
- Tests POSS marker format
- Tests PRONOMINAL_PREFIXES dictionary

Coverage maintained at 99.998% (831,415/831,431 tokens)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement -sak CAUS/BENF distinction based on verb stem form

Based on Otsuka (2009): Form I + -sak = causative, Form II + -sak = benefactive

Changes:
- Added Form II stem tracking in stem selection (is_form_ii_stem flag)
- Added .II marker to Form II verb glosses (e.g., 'do.II', 'see.II')
- Implemented -sak disambiguation: BENF after Form II stems, CAUS otherwise
- Updated suffix handling in three code paths:
  1. Main stem extraction (lines 19655-19669)
  2. STRIPPABLE_SUFFIXES loop (lines 19706-19715, 19739-19741)
  3. suffix_glosses recursive parsing (lines 19859-19871)

Updated compound entries to use Form II + BENF:
- muhsak: see-CAUS → see.II-BENF
- cihsak: say-CAUS → say.II-BENF
- neihsak: have-CAUS → have.II-BENF
- theihsak: know-CAUS → know.II-BENF

Added tests/test_sak_caus_benf.py:
- Tests Form II + sak → BENF (7 verbs)
- Tests Form I + sak → CAUS
- Tests .II marker presence
- Tests lexicalized compounds (musak='show')
- Tests VERB_STEM_PAIRS dictionary structure

Coverage maintained at 99.998% (831,415/831,431 tokens)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 13 literature review reports for Tedim Chin grammar

Created comprehensive literature reviews compiling previous scholarship
(Henderson 1965, Zam Ngaih Cing 2017/2018, Singh 2018, Otsuka 2009-2024)
on each grammar topic.

Reports created (~113KB total):
- 05-verb-01-stems-lit.md: Verb stem alternation (Form I/II)
- 05-verb-05-aspect-lit.md: Aspect markers (perfective, progressive, etc.)
- 05-verb-06-directional-lit.md: Directional affixes (-suk, -kia, etc.)
- 05-verb-09-valency-lit.md: Causative/benefactive -sak distinction
- 03-noun-05-postpositions-lit.md: Case markers (-in ERG, -a INSTR, etc.)
- 06-func-01-pronouns-lit.md: Personal, possessive, demonstrative pronouns
- 06-func-02-demonstratives-lit.md: Proximal/distal deixis
- 06-func-03-numerals-lit.md: Cardinals, ordinals, distributives
- 06-func-04-negation-lit.md: Negation markers (-kei, -lou)
- 04-np-07-possession-lit.md: Possessive constructions, tonal marking
- 08-clause-03-subordination-lit.md: Subordination strategies
- 09-sent-01-interrogatives-lit.md: Question formation
- 10-disc-01-sentence-final-lit.md: Sentence-final particles

Each report follows standard format with chronological source review,
synthesis tables comparing across languages, and open research questions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

Also adds save verification and error logging to
`event-diagnostics.log`.

## Tests
6 new tests covering:
- Orchestrator role detection by name pattern (with suffix variants)
- Group reconstruction from scattered sessions
- Multiple scattered teams handling
- No false positives on regular session names
- No-op when groups are already correct

All 55 organization tests pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
